subject: Sociology
title: Rich Nations and Poor Nations
description: A micro-lesson that makes Rich Nations and Poor Nations usable.

hook.text: A child born in Norway will live 30 years longer than one born in Sierra Leone. Same planet, vastly different fates.

buildup.text: Global inequality is the gap in wealth, health, and opportunity between nations. It's staggering in scale.

discovery.text: The richest 20% of the world's population consumes over 75% of its resources. The poorest 20% gets about 1.5%.

twist.text: Colonialism extracted wealth from Africa and Asia for centuries. Today's map of poverty follows old colonial borders.

climax.text: Global inequality isn't natural. It's the product of conquest, exploitation, and trade rules set by powerful nations.

punchline.text: The global order wasn't designed to be fair. It was designed to be profitable.

quiz.question: What historical factor shaped today's global inequality?
